core_mechanism: self-consistent mean-field variational minimization over a BCS-type paired-state ansatz coupled to a coherent (condensate) field, yielding a symmetry-broken ground state and a finite-temperature critical threshold whose order parameter scales with the number of constituents
brief_reason: The abstract solves the system by a selfconsistent procedure with a trial function combining a coherent photon field and a BCS pairing function, minimizing the ground-state energy and extracting a critical temperature — the hallmark of self-consistent mean-field pairing theory.
